reasoning: The flagged macOS uninstall script isn't executed during Arch Linux package build/install. All PKGBUILD operations use verified upstream sources and standard packaging patterns with no malicious behavior.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged src/pandoc-3.10/macos/uninstall-pandoc.pl as unsafe despite it being macOS-specific and unused in Arch context
coverage_issues: Both models skipped pandoc-bin-3.10.tar.gz binary (acceptable for AUR audit)
qwen reviewed wasm patches but missed Linux/macOS build scripts reviewed by deepseek
false_positives: uninstall-pandoc.pl: macOS script incorrectly flagged as unsafe (never executed in Arch context)
Privilege escalation claim: Requires macOS admin rights but irrelevant to Arch build
learnings: Improve OS-context awareness: De-prioritize OS-specific files (e.g., macOS scripts) for Linux packages
Enhance risk contextualization: Build/install hooks should override irrelevant file findings
Optimize file selection: Prioritize PKGBUILD, install hooks, and build scripts over patches/web assets
Reduce false positives: Train models to distinguish between actual build-time risks and unused auxiliary scripts
